text: Many students believe that spending more hours at their desk will lead to better exam results.
However, research shows that the way you study matters more than the time you spend.
First, set a clear deadline for each topic and stick to it.
Break large subjects into smaller parts and tackle them one by one.
Taking short breaks helps your brain stay fresh and productive.
Another useful approach is to evaluate what you already know before revising new material.
This helps you focus your energy where it is truly needed.
Some students overlook the importance of sleep, but resting well the night before an exam is crucial.
Finally, do not neglect practice tests.
They demonstrate how well you have prepared and highlight areas that still need attention.
With discipline and a logical plan, any student can improve their results.
